moving-around.label: Moving Around with cd
moving-around.summary: Use cd, .., ., and ~ to move between folders safely.

quiz1.title: Purpose of the cd Command
quiz1.prompt: What is the primary purpose of the `cd` command in Linux?

help.concept: The `cd` command is used to change the current working directory in the terminal.
help.hint_1: The `cd` command helps you navigate the file system.

options.a: To list files in a directory

quiz2.prompt: Which of the following commands will move you to the home directory?

quiz3.title: Order of Commands to Navigate Directories
quiz3.prompt: Arrange the commands in the order you would use them to navigate from `/home/user` to `/home/user/Documents/Projects`.
quiz3.hint: Think about moving step by step through the directories.

help.concept: To navigate directories, use `cd` followed by the directory name in sequence.
help.hint_1: Start from `/home/user` and move into `Documents`, then `Projects`.
help.hint_2: The sequence should reflect moving into each directory one by one.

quiz4.title: Using Relative Paths
quiz4.prompt: Complete the command to move up one directory level from the current directory.
quiz4.hint: Use the relative path symbol for moving up one level.

help.concept: The `..` symbol is used to move up one directory level.
help.hint_1: The `..` symbol represents the parent directory.
help.hint_2: Use `cd ..` to move up one level.

quiz5.title: Navigating to Home Directory

cd-enters-folders.title: `cd` changes the current folder
cd-enters-folders.bodyMarkdown:
`cd` moves your terminal into another folder. After you move, run `pwd` so you can confirm where you landed.

Worked example:
```bash
cd city-tour
pwd
```

Line by line: the first line moves into the `city-tour` folder. The second line prints the new current path so you can verify the move.

dot-dot-parent.title: `..` means the parent folder
dot-dot-parent.bodyMarkdown:
Use `cd ..` when you need to move up one folder from your current location.

Worked example:
```bash
cd station
cd ..
```

Step by step: the first command moves into `station`. The second command moves back to the parent folder that contains `station`.

home-shortcut.title: Longer paths help you move in one jump
home-shortcut.bodyMarkdown: A path like `city-tour/station/platform` lets you move through nested folders in one command. Use `pwd` or `ls` after moving if you want to double-check your location.
